ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-13-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary Mayo, via ZOOM, for Leland Valentine, Administrator.

The court points to the recent filings of receipts in this matter. Ms. Mayo reports regarding the issues with previously filed receipts. The court and Ms. Mayo discuss her request for extraordinary fees and offers support for her request. The court confirms with Ms. Mayo that the estate is ready to close.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Petition is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Preliminary Distribution, Settling First Account, Partial Allowance Of Administrators Commission And Costs And For Partial Allowance Of Ordinary Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jul-15-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary Mayo, via ZOOM, for Leland Valentine, Administrator; Avid Alavi, via ZOOM, Guardian Ad Litem for David Valentine Jr., Beneficiary.

Ms. Mayo begins by reporting that the ex parte application to waive bond was filed on July 6, 2021. Ms. Mayo then reports on the partial Inventory and Appraisal she previously filed and that she is waiting for notification from the U.S. Treasury regarding the status of treasury bonds and she hopes to file a Final Inventory and Appraisal as soon as that matter is clarified. The court then advises Ms. Mayo that it is leaning towards granting a 50% distribution. Both Ms. Mayo and Ms. Alavi advise that they agree with the court's tentative ruling regarding granting a 50% distribution. Ms. Alavi reports that a DNA test has confirmed David Valentine Jr.'s relationship to decedent. Consequently, a passport has been issued and an application for a social security number is pending with the U.S. Embassy for David Valentine, Jr. The court then queries Ms. Mayo if she expects to file waivers of accounting. Ms. Mayo does not and is advised by the court that it will be expecting an accounting to be filed, accordingly.

After additional discussion, the Amended Petition for Preliminary Distribution, etc., is submitted. The Status on Petition to Waive Bond is taken off calendar. The Status on Inventory and Appraisal is continued to October 6, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204. A Status on Close of Estate and Status on 1St Accounting are also set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Balmy. Not reported.

Petition For Preliminary Distribution, Settling First Account, Partial Allowance Of Administrators Commission And Costs And For Partial Allowance Of Ordinary Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jun-16-2021 Continued To Jul-14-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jun-17-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary Mayo, via ZOOM, counsel for Leland Valentine, Petitioner.

The court begins by querying counsel as to how David Valentine will receive his share of funds from the Trust. Counsel reports that a sub-trust will be created for David and the funds will be distributed to him from that sub-trust. The court then inquires as to a corrected Inventory and Appraisal (I&A). Counsel reports that the two partial I&A's are correct and that she is in the process of preparing a final I&A to submit once the sale of real property is final, which is likely soon. The court advised counsel that it will be adding a status of I&A, accordingly. The court also reminds counsel that there are currently no waivers of bond and instructs counsel to file an application to waive bond, which she may be able to do ex parte.

After additional discussion, The Status on Close of Estate is taken off calendar. The Accounting Status Petition is taken under submission. The Amended Petition for Preliminary Distribution, etc., is continued to July 14, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. A Status on Petition to Waive Bond and a Status of Inventory and Appraisal is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar May-05-2021 Continued To Jun-16-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary Mayo, via ZOOM, counsel for Leland Valentine, Administrator.

At the outset, the court queries counsel with regard to the distribution of the more than 280 thousand dollars was received. Counsel reports that the distribution is mentioned as an exhibit in the amended petition filed on October 19, 2020. The court then turns to the issue of Ms. Alavi not being appointed as Guardian Ad Litem in this matter, which the court would prefer for distribution purposes. After additional discussion, the court advises counsel to work on the issues including but not limited to the Inventory and Appraisal and appointment of Guardian ad Litem.

After additional discussion, the Probate Accounting Status Petition and Amended Petition for Preliminary Distribution are continued to June 16, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-21-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary Mayo for Leland Valentino (Petitioner).

The Court noted that Mr. Jay's Client withdrew her petition. Ms. Mayo stated that she has not received any objections. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). Bond is set at $300,000.00. Status on close of estate is set for is May 19, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-20-2019 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-23-2019 Continued To Nov-20-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Mary Mayo for Leland Valentine. Gilbert Jay for Karen Ann Valentine (Petitioner/Objector).

The Court stated that the parties had left optimistically after the last hearing to work on an agreement as to who would administer the estate. Ms. Mayo stated that was not her understanding, that Judge Quinn is reluctant to make any decisions until an administrator is appointed, and that they are submitting on their petition. Mr. Jay addressed his client locating the hand-printed and hand-signed will. The Court stated that no holographic will was on file. Mr. Jay stated that the signature is the decedent's, that they can't prove that the printing is hers, that he doesn't think that they'll be able to prove it, that he may be willing to withdraw, that the only major asset would be the expectancy from the Mother's trust, and that the leading decedent misappropriated trust assets. The Court stated that those are all issues in the trust matter and addressed withdrawing the will as probatable. Mr. Jay stated that his client's objection is not withdrawn, that he was contacted by Sam, that Sam objected to Leland, and that he refused to represent Sam until Karen has withdrawn. Brief discussions are held. Mr. Jay stated that he would like to file an objection on Sam's behalf. At the conclusion of discussions, the Court continued both the Petition for Letters of Administration for Leland Valentine (filed 7/23/19) and the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary for Karen Valentine (filed 8/1/19) to November 20, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Any objections are to be filed by November 13, 2019. The Court stated that if no objections are filed then Leland's petition would be granted or that it would look at any objections that are on file. The Court urged the parties to talk to each other prior to the next hearing.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: M. Nuque. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-23-2019 Continued To Nov-20-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-11-2019 Continued To Oct-23-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gilbert Jay for Karen Ann Valentine (Petitioner).
Mary Mayo for Leland Valentine (original Petitioner).

The Court noted that it does not have proof of the holographic will. Ms. Mayo stated that the trust matter is only related in that Patricia Valentine was the first Trustee in that trust, that the estate is now a quarter beneficiary of that trust, that funds will be coming into the estate from that trust, that they have had discussion and that they may be able to come to some agreement. Mr. Jay stated that he has not been involved in the trust matter, and that if Ms. Valentine is appointed she will have to step into the shoes of the Decedent and in the trust litigation. Ms. Mayo stated that Leland would prepare an accounting for the Decedent as the first trustee, that the accounting will be before the Court and that there will be transparency. Mr. Jay raised the issue of surcharge against the Decedent. The Petition for Letters of Administration, and the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary are continued to October 23, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to discuss the matter, and to determine who will serve, whether they will have to bring in a third party and how the trust relates to this estate.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
